spec_match.rationale: Demonstrates font-variant-emoji values per the css-fonts-4 spec; CSS.supports probe confirms parser acceptance.

interactive.rationale: Live class swap drives the rendered presentation; real toggle, not a static card.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature card promises per-element override of platform default — delivered.

edge_cases.rationale: No demonstration of dual-presentation Unicode characters (e.g. U+2764, U+2600) that flip differently than full-emoji codepoints. No edge case showing what happens when an emoji font is missing from the platform.

references.rationale: See also includes https://chromestatus.com/feature/6566092561973248 and the css-fonts-4 spec link.

openQuestions.title: Add dual-presentation glyphs to the test string
openQuestions.detail: Codepoints like U+2764 (heart) and U+2600 (sun) have explicit dual presentations. The current demo uses a fixed string; let the visitor edit it and include some dual-presentation chars by default to show the variant kicking in.

openQuestions.title: Show what happens with U+FE0E / U+FE0F overrides
openQuestions.detail: Variation selectors compose with font-variant-emoji. Currently the demo doesn't surface this layering. Add a 'with VS-15' / 'with VS-16' control so the visitor sees how spec precedence resolves.

summary: Clean base demo with the canonical CSS.supports probe and live toggle. Could probe more spec corners around dual-presentation codepoints and variation selectors.
